children.text:
Spiritual Adoption of a Conceived Child is an individual intercessory prayer undertaken privately or in public. It depends on taking a 9-month long spiritual care of an unknown child, whose life is endangered with abortion. A person undertaking this commitment, every day recites one decade of the rosary, considers a mystery of the life of Jesus and Mary along with the special prayer for the adopted child. A voluntary sacrifice can be added to the prayer, i.e. fasting, charity, deeds of mercy.

According to the words of Jesus Christ: 'This kind (of spirits) can come forth by nothing, but by prayer and fasting' (Mk 9,29). This is why there is a need to create a multitude of the protectors of human life. With a holy rosary in their hand, they stand against the great evil that is the death of unborn, weakest and helpless children.Entrusting God with the conceived life is important, especially when the life is endangered becuase of health issues or parents struggling to accpet it.

Apart from taking care of and saving a particular child endangered with abortion, the Spiritual Adoption prayer bares numerous fruits. It heals the hurtings left in the hearts of these people who decided for and helped with the abortion. It may also be a regular family prayer of the parents and children. Spiritual Adoption is a gift of a spiritual motherhood for elder people and a gift of parenthood for these couples who are awaiting their child to be conceived and born. It is also a perfect preparation to the sacrament of marriage.

 What you should know about the Spiritual Adoption of the Conceived Child:

- Anyone can start the Spiritual Adoption: laymen, women, men, the sick, children with their parents' care and people livin in the non-sacramental relationships.

- Spiritual Adoption can be undertaken many times, under the condition that the previous commitments were fulfilled.

- Spiritual Adoption can be undertaken everywhere, but the vow must be made each time. Nonetheless, a solemn form is advisable because of the importance of this prayer.
-  A short break in the prayer of Spiritual Adoption is not a sin. 
- A long break breaches the Adoption. The promise must be then renewed and try to keep it. A conscious ignoring of a vow made to God is a sin.
